stories.title: Best iPhone Charger: Cable, Wireless, MagSafe, and More
bullets: A guide was published detailing the best iPhone chargers, including cable, wireless, and MagSafe options.
The article emphasizes compatibility and efficiency, catering to various user needs, from heavy users to casual ones.
Choosing the right charger can enhance user experience, reflecting the growing importance of mobile device accessories.

stories.title: Australians will call ‘bullshit’ on green energy without clearer benefits, Rudd warns
bullets: Former Australian Prime Minister Kevin Rudd warned that public support for green energy policies could collapse without clear benefits.
Rudd criticized Donald Trump's cuts to green industry support, stating Australians would see the transition as 'bullshit' if it lacks tangible outcomes.
His comments emphasize the need for effective communication and results in climate policy to maintain public trust and support.

stories.title: Canada Says Its Gold Is Traceable and Clean. So We Traced It.
bullets: An investigation revealed that Canada's gold supply chain leads to a Colombian mine controlled by a drug cartel.
Despite claims of traceability and cleanliness, the findings raise serious concerns about ethical sourcing and environmental impact.
This revelation could damage Canada's reputation in the global gold market, prompting calls for stricter oversight.

bullets: Summary unavailable

stories.title: STAT+: Intellia says CRISPR-based treatment for rare disease reduced swelling attacks in pivotal trial
bullets: Intellia's CRISPR-based treatment for a rare disease showed promising results, reducing swelling attacks in a pivotal trial.
The treatment's effectiveness raises questions about its appeal as a one-time solution for patients and healthcare providers.
This breakthrough could reshape treatment approaches in genetic disorders, emphasizing the potential of CRISPR technology.

stories.title: The Tech Bros Are All In on Zyn
bullets: Tech workers are increasingly using nicotine pouches, viewing them as effective stimulants for productivity and focus.
These pouches are gaining popularity in the tech industry, with users praising their convenience and brain-boosting effects.
The trend highlights a shift in workplace habits, reflecting broader cultural attitudes towards nicotine consumption.

stories.title: STAT+: Oruka’s long-acting psoriasis therapy posts strong results in mid-stage study
bullets: Oruka Therapeutics reported that 63% of patients achieved complete skin clearance with their long-acting psoriasis therapy.
The mid-stage study results indicate significant potential for this injectable treatment in managing plaque psoriasis.
These findings could lead to advancements in psoriasis care, offering new hope for patients seeking effective therapies.

stories.title: Ever wondered how NASA astronauts tell time?
bullets: NASA astronauts on the Artemis II Mission wear specialized watches designed for timekeeping in space.
These timepieces are engineered to function accurately in the unique conditions of space travel and zero gravity.
Understanding how astronauts manage time enhances public interest in space exploration and the technology involved.

stories.title: Iran ‘offers to end chokehold on Strait of Hormuz’; Israel ‘kills three Hizbullah members’
bullets: Iran has proposed to ease its control over the Strait of Hormuz, while Israel reportedly killed three Hizbullah members.
European Commission president Ursula von der Leyen stated that it is premature to lift sanctions on Iran.
These developments could impact regional stability and international relations, particularly concerning oil shipping routes.
